question: [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: supplyCollateral / isHealthy] Can an unprivileged borrower, using a fee-on-transfer collateral token, under the precondition that the market accepts a collateral whose ERC20 transfer charges a percentage fee, trigger supplyCollateral(assets=X) so that SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom pulls only X-fee into the contract while position.collateral[i] records X, then call take to borrow against the inflated recorded collateral, causing isHealthy to compute maxDebt using the over-recorded X rather than the actual X-fee held, violating the invariant that only unhealthy accounts should be liquidatable and that invalid borrowers must not stay healthy, causing scoped impact: invalid borrower remains healthy because oracle-priced collateral value is computed on phantom collateral units never held by the protocol? Proof idea: deploy a fee-on-transfer ERC20 as collateral, fuzz fee percentage in [1, 50]%, supply collateral, borrow to the oracle-derived limit, assert isHealthy returns true while actual token balance of Midnight is less than position.collateral[i] * oracle_price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that a borrower previously supplied a fee-on-transfer collateral token so that position.collateral[collateralIndex] > actual contract balance of that token, trigger liquidate(seizedAssets = position.collateral[collateralIndex]) which computes repaidUnits via oracle price and then calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) with seizedAssets exceeding the actual token balance held, causing the transfer to revert and the liquidation to fail, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a genuinely unhealthy position reverts unexpectedly due to oracle-derived seizedAssets exceeding actual balance? Proof idea: fuzz fee percentage and collateral amount, assert that liquidate reverts with an ERC20 insufficient-balance error when seizedAssets > balanceOf(Midnight, collateralToken).

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / IOracle.price] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the oracle for the liquidated collateral returns 0 and the liquidator passes seizedAssets > 0 with repaidUnits = 0, trigger the liquidate path where repaidUnits = seizedAssets.mulDivUp(0, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif) = 0, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) to succeed while SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), 0) transfers nothing, violating the invariant that every liquidation must require the liquidator to repay correct debt value, causing scoped impact: liquidator seizes collateral for zero loan token repayment, constituting direct loss of borrower collateral? Proof idea: deploy oracle returning 0, fuzz seizedAssets in [1, type(uint128).max], assert repaidUnits == 0 and collateral is transferred to liquidator without any loan token inflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / IOracle.price] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the oracle for the liquidated collateral returns 0 and the liquidator passes repaidUnits > 0 with seizedAssets = 0, trigger the liquidate path where seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, 0) which performs division by zero and reverts, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a genuinely unhealthy position reverts unexpectedly when the oracle returns 0 for the target collateral? Proof idea: deploy oracle returning 0, call liquidate with repaidUnits > 0 and seizedAssets = 0, assert revert due to division by zero in mulDivDown.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: isHealthy / withdrawCollateral / SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that an activated collateral oracle returns a price P such that position.collateral[i] * P overflows uint256 in the mulDivDown call inside isHealthy, trigger withdrawCollateral which calls isHealthy and causes an arithmetic overflow revert before SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer is ever reached, violating the invariant that healthy accounts must not have their withdrawals frozen, causing scoped impact: valid borrower with a healthy position cannot withdraw any collateral because isHealthy reverts unexpectedly due to oracle price overflow? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price near type(uint256).max / collateral_amount, assert withdrawCollateral reverts with overflow when collateral * price > type(uint256).max.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / isHealthy loop / IOracle.price] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that a borrower has activated multiple collaterals and one non-liquidated collateral oracle returns a price causing collateral[i] * price to overflow uint256 in the mulDivDown inside the liquidate oracle loop, trigger liquidate which iterates all activated collaterals and overflows on the non-target collateral, causing the entire liquidation to revert, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a genuinely unhealthy multi-collateral borrower reverts unexpectedly due to oracle overflow on a non-liquidated collateral? Proof idea: create multi-collateral market, set one oracle to return near-max price, fuzz collateral amounts, assert liquidate reverts with overflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: isHealthy / withdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that one of the borrower's activated collateral oracles is a contract that reverts on price(), trigger withdrawCollateral which calls isHealthy which iterates all activated collaterals via collateralBitmap and reverts when the reverting oracle is called, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer to never be reached, violating the invariant that healthy accounts must not have their withdrawals frozen, causing scoped impact: valid borrower with a healthy position cannot withdraw any collateral because isHealthy reverts due to a single reverting oracle among multiple activated collaterals? Proof idea: create multi-collateral market, deploy reverting oracle for one collateral, supply both collaterals, assert withdrawCollateral reverts even when the non-reverting collateral alone covers the debt.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / IOracle.price multi-collateral loop] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that a borrower has activated multiple collaterals and one non-liquidated collateral oracle reverts on price(), trigger liquidate which iterates all activated collaterals and reverts when the reverting oracle is called, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a genuinely unhealthy borrower reverts unexpectedly because a single reverting oracle among multiple activated collaterals blocks the entire liquidation? Proof idea: create multi-collateral market, deploy reverting oracle for one collateral, make borrower unhealthy on another collateral, assert liquidate reverts.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: isHealthy / liquidate] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that a multi-collateral borrower has one activated collateral whose oracle returns 0, trigger liquidate where the oracle loop computes maxDebt += collateral[i] * 0 / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD = 0 for that collateral, causing maxDebt to be underestimated relative to the true collateral value, so that originalDebt > maxDebt evaluates to true even though the borrower is actually healthy when the zero-priced collateral is correctly valued, violating the invariant that healthy accounts must not become liquidatable, causing scoped impact: valid borrower is wrongfully liquidated because oracle returning 0 for one collateral makes the position appear unhealthy? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amounts and oracle prices, assert that if all oracles returned correct non-zero prices the position would be healthy, but with one oracle returning 0 the liquidate NotLiquidatable check passes.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: supplyCollateral / isHealthy / liquidate] Can an unprivileged borrower, using a rebasing collateral token that rebases downward after supply, under the precondition that position.collateral[i] records the pre-rebase amount while the contract's actual token balance decreases, trigger a state where isHealthy computes maxDebt using the stale over-recorded collateral amount while the oracle prices the actual token, causing the borrower to appear healthier than they are, violating the invariant that invalid borrowers must not stay healthy, causing scoped impact: undercollateralized borrower remains non-liquidatable because health check uses phantom collateral units that no longer exist in the contract? Proof idea: deploy rebasing ERC20 as collateral, supply collateral, trigger rebase-down, assert isHealthy returns true while actual collateral value at oracle price is below debt.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that a borrower's collateral token has self-destructed (code.length == 0) after the borrower supplied it, trigger liquidate which computes seizedAssets via oracle price and then calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) which reverts with NoCode() because the token contract no longer exists, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a genuinely unhealthy borrower reverts unexpectedly because SafeTransferLib.NoCode() is thrown for a self-destructed collateral token? Proof idea: deploy self-destructable ERC20, supply collateral, self-destruct the token, assert liquidate reverts with NoCode().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: withdrawCollateral / SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that the collateral token has self-destructed (code.length == 0) after the borrower supplied it, trigger withdrawCollateral which passes the isHealthy check (oracle may still return a price from a separate oracle contract) and then calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) which reverts with NoCode(), violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen outside valid protocol rules, causing scoped impact: valid borrower with zero debt cannot withdraw their collateral because SafeTransferLib.NoCode() is thrown for a self-destructed collateral token? Proof idea: deploy self-destructable ERC20, supply collateral, repay all debt, self-destruct the token, assert withdrawCollateral reverts with NoCode().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the collateral token returns false on transfer (non-reverting false-return ERC20), trigger liquidate which computes seizedAssets via oracle price and then calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) which reverts with TransferReturnedFalse(), violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a genuinely unhealthy borrower reverts unexpectedly because the collateral token returns false on transfer? Proof idea: deploy false-return ERC20 as collateral, make borrower unhealthy, assert liquidate reverts with TransferReturnedFalse().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: withdrawCollateral / SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer] Can an unprivileged borrower, under the precondition that the collateral token returns false on transfer, trigger withdrawCollateral which passes the isHealthy check and then calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, assets) which reverts with TransferReturnedFalse(), violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen outside valid protocol rules, causing scoped impact: valid borrower with a healthy position cannot withdraw collateral because the token returns false on transfer? [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer zero-value] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the oracle for the liquidated collateral returns a very small price P such that seizedAssets.mulDivUp(P, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, lif) rounds to 0 for a small seizedAssets input, trigger liquidate where repaidUnits = 0 and SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), 0) is called, and if the loan token rejects zero-value transfers, the liquidation reverts, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts unexpectedly when oracle price is near-zero and loan token rejects zero-value transferFrom? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price in [1, 1000], fuzz seizedAssets in [1, 100], assert repaidUnits rounds to 0 and liquidate reverts if loan token rejects zero transfers.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer zero-value collateral] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the oracle for the liquidated collateral returns a very large price P such that repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, P) rounds to 0 for a small repaidUnits input, trigger liquidate where seizedAssets = 0 and SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, 0) is called, and if the collateral token rejects zero-value transfers, the liquidation reverts, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts unexpectedly when oracle price is very high and collateral token rejects zero-value transfer? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price near type(uint256).max / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, fuzz repaidUnits in [1, 100], assert seizedAssets rounds to 0 and liquidate reverts if collateral token rejects zero transfers.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: isHealthy / mulDivDown rounding] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that a borrower's position is at the exact health boundary (debt == maxDebt) and the oracle price is at a value where mulDivDown rounding in maxDebt += collateral.mulDivDown(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivDown(lltv, WAD) rounds down by 1, trigger liquidate where originalDebt > maxDebt evaluates to true due to rounding, violating the invariant that healthy accounts must not become liquidatable due to rounding, causing scoped impact: valid borrower at exact health boundary is wrongfully liquidated because oracle-price-based mulDivDown rounding makes maxDebt one unit too low? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amounts and oracle prices near the health boundary, assert that for any (collateral, price, lltv) triple where debt == floor(collateral * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD), liquidate does not succeed.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / RCF maxRepaid / IOracle.price] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the oracle price for the liquidated collateral is at a boundary where (_position.debt - maxDebt).mulDivUp(WAD * WAD, WAD * WAD - lif * lltv) overflows uint256 because lif * lltv approaches WAD * WAD, trigger liquidate where the maxRepaid computation overflows and the RCF check either reverts or produces an incorrect maxRepaid, violating the invariant that repay amount must be capped by the recovery close factor, causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts unexpectedly due to overflow in RCF computation when oracle-derived lif and lltv product approaches WAD^2? Proof idea: fuzz lltv in allowed tiers and lif at maxLif boundary, assert that WAD * WAD - lif * lltv > 0 for all valid (lltv, lif) pairs and that maxRepaid computation never overflows.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / badDebt / IOracle.price] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the oracle returns a price such that collateral.mulDivUp(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, maxLif) overflows uint256 in the badDebt computation loop, trigger liquidate which reverts due to overflow in the badDebt = badDebt.zeroFloorSub(collateral.mulDivUp(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE).mulDivUp(WAD, maxLif)) line, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a genuinely unhealthy borrower reverts unexpectedly due to oracle price overflow in the bad-debt computation? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price near type(uint256).max / collateral_amount, assert that mulDivUp(price, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE) does not overflow for any valid oracle price.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: supplyCollateral / isHealthy / decimals mismatch] Can an unprivileged market creator, under the precondition that the market is created with a collateral token having 6 decimals and an oracle that returns a price scaled for 18-decimal tokens (i.e., price is 1e36 * 1e18 / 1e6 = 1e48 per token unit), trigger supplyCollateral with a small amount of 6-decimal collateral, then take to borrow, causing isHealthy to compute maxDebt = collateral_units * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD where price is 1e12 times too large, making the borrower appear massively overcollateralized, violating the invariant that invalid borrowers must not stay healthy, causing scoped impact: undercollateralized borrower remains non-liquidatable because oracle price is not adjusted for token decimals? Proof idea: deploy 6-decimal ERC20, deploy oracle returning 18-decimal-scaled price, fuzz borrow amount, assert isHealthy returns true when actual USD value of collateral is below debt.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: isHealthy / withdrawCollateral / decimals mismatch] Can an unprivileged market creator, under the precondition that the market is created with a collateral token having 27 decimals and an oracle that returns a price scaled for 18-decimal tokens (i.e., price is 1e36 * 1e18 / 1e27 = 1e27 per token unit), trigger a state where isHealthy computes maxDebt = collateral_units * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD where price is 1e9 times too small, making the borrower appear undercollateralized, violating the invariant that healthy accounts must not become liquidatable due to oracle edge cases, causing scoped impact: valid borrower with sufficient collateral is wrongfully liquidatable because oracle price is not adjusted for 27-decimal token? Proof idea: deploy 27-decimal ERC20, deploy oracle returning 18-decimal-scaled price, assert isHealthy returns false when actual USD value of collateral exceeds debt.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom / loan token false return] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the loan token returns false on transferFrom (non-reverting false-return ERC20), trigger liquidate which computes repaidUnits via oracle price, updates all state (collateral bitmap, debt, withdrawable, lossFactor), emits the Liquidate event, calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransfer(collateralToken, receiver, seizedAssets) successfully, and then calls SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom(loanToken, payer, address(this), repaidUnits) which reverts with TransferFromReturnedFalse(), causing the entire transaction to revert and roll back all state, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation of a genuinely unhealthy borrower reverts unexpectedly because the loan token returns false on transferFrom? Proof idea: deploy false-return loan token, make borrower unhealthy, assert liquidate reverts with TransferFromReturnedFalse().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: take / isHealthy / SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom] Can an unprivileged taker, under the precondition that the seller (maker) has debt and one of the seller's activated collateral oracles returns a price causing overflow in isHealthy, trigger take which calls isHealthy(offer.market, id, seller) at line 476 and reverts due to oracle price overflow, causing SafeTransferLib.safeTransferFrom to never be reached, violating the invariant that user funds must not be frozen outside valid protocol rules, causing scoped impact: valid take that would reduce seller debt reverts unexpectedly because oracle price overflow in isHealthy blocks the SellerIsLiquidatable check? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price near type(uint256).max / collateral_amount for seller's collateral, assert take reverts with overflow when seller has debt.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / toUint128 / IOracle.price] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the oracle returns a price P such that collateral.mulDivDown(P, ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE) > type(uint128).max, trigger liquidate where the maxDebt accumulation does not itself overflow uint256 but a downstream toUint128 cast on a derived value overflows, violating the invariant that unhealthy positions must remain liquidatable, causing scoped impact: liquidation reverts unexpectedly due to CastOverflow in UtilsLib.toUint128 when oracle price causes an intermediate value to exceed uint128 bounds? Proof idea: fuzz oracle price and collateral amount such that collateral * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE > type(uint128).max, assert liquidate reverts with CastOverflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/SafeTransferLib.sol -> Scope: High Oracle edge-case bug causing valid borrowers to become liquidatable, invalid borrowers to stay healthy, or withdrawals/liquidations to revert unexpectedly'] [Function: liquidate / post-maturity lif / IOracle.price] Can an unprivileged liquidator, under the precondition that the market is post-maturity and the oracle for the liquidated collateral returns 0, trigger liquidate in postMaturityMode=true where lif is computed as min(maxLif, WAD + (maxLif - WAD) * elapsed / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF) and then seizedAssets = repaidUnits.mulDivDown(lif, WAD).mulDivDown(ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE, 0) performs division by zero, violating the invariant that overdue debt after maturity must be liquidatable, causing scoped impact: post-maturity liquidation reverts unexpectedly due to division by zero when oracle returns 0 for the liquidated collateral? Proof idea: warp past maturity, deploy oracle returning 0, call liquidate with repaidUnits > 0 and postMaturityMode=true, assert revert.
